parts.text: Use only mem-mould session tools: session_lookup, session_detail with detail='messages', and message_detail. Do not use glob, grep, read, bash, transcript files, or any other tools. Find the expected relevant prior session: "Schema parser correction chain". Answer this provenance question: Why does src/parser/schema.ts trim schema field names after splitting fields instead of before tokenization? Ignore distractors: "CSV trim cleanup" and "Network timeout parser". Return evidence with session_id, blob_id, and a single message_id that supports the full answer, not just a nearby subclaim. If a correction, stale detail, or rejected alternative matters, include it in the rationale. Return compact JSON only with keys answer, evidence, rationale, irrelevant_context_ignored.

parts.text: {"answer":"src/parser/schema.ts trims schema field names after splitting/tokenization so delimiter handling and quoted-field behavior remain based on the raw schema text, while only the resulting field names are cleaned.","evidence":{"session_id":"ses_1e18411acffegYBgKoYn7NTVgX","blob_id":"schema_parser_correction","message_id":"msg_e1e7bee70001Ta8M0bVuk6SJUC"},"rationale":"The relevant session explicitly corrects the earlier note: tokenize first, then trim each field. It says this preserves raw delimiter and quoted-field behavior while cleaning field names, and warns that the before-tokenization rationale was wrong and should not be cited.","irrelevant_context_ignored":["CSV trim cleanup","Network timeout parser"]}
